machine.cpu: Intel(R) Xeon(R) Platinum 8480+ (Sapphire Rapids), 26 cores / 52 threads, 1 socket
machine.caches: as reported by sysfs in this guest: L1d 32 KiB, L1i 32 KiB, L2 4 MiB unified per core, L3 16 MiB
machine.scheduler: EEVDF (default since 6.6); cgroup v2 unified hierarchy
machine.virtualization: KVM guest (hypervisor flag set, VT-x exposed); numbers include guest-exit effects
machine.storage: virtio-blk /dev/vda, 5.5 TB, mq-deadline available but scheduler set to none; backing device type not visible from the guest
machine.notes: Shared multi-tenant virtual machine. Medians of repeated runs; treat as order-of-magnitude engineering numbers, not microarchitectural truth.

syscall_vs_call.method: 3e6 iterations per loop, pinned to one logical CPU, CLOCK_MONOTONIC; the user function call goes through a volatile function pointer so it cannot be inlined; python rows are 2e6-iteration loops of the stdlib wrappers on CPython 3.10

socketpair_epoll.method: 1-byte ping-pong over AF_UNIX SOCK_STREAM socketpair, 100k round trips, both processes pinned; same-core values are medians of 3 runs, cross-core single runs with wide variance on this virtualized host

tlb_reach.method: anonymous mmap, memset-faulted, pinned; random order is a single Sattolo-cycle pointer chase with one 8-byte load per 4 KiB page, 20e6 dependent loads; THP via madvise(MADV_HUGEPAGE) after prctl(PR_SET_THP_DISABLE, 0) because this host starts processes with THP disabled by prctl; AnonHugePages in smaps_rollup confirmed the full region was huge-page backed

io_uring_warm_batching.method: 1e6 random 4 KiB reads from a page-cache-warm 1 GiB ext4 file, raw io_uring syscalls, medians of 6 runs; spread across runs was roughly 2x on this shared host

context_switch.method: 1-byte pipe ping-pong, 199k round trips after 1k warmup, both peers pinned to the same logical CPU so each round trip forces two switches

context_switch_working_set.method: same ping-pong, but each peer touches 512 random 64-byte lines in its own buffer per turn; solo_turn_ns is the same work with no peer

fork_cow.note: child _exit(0) immediately; parent times fork() return only
fork_cow.method: parent times fork() return only; the child calls _exit(0) immediately. cow_fault_ns is a separate child writing one byte into every inherited page. THP disabled with MADV_NOHUGEPAGE so page-table work is visible

page_faults.method: 512 MiB anonymous mapping, one store per 4 KiB page; fault counts read from getrusage(RUSAGE_SELF).ru_minflt; the pointer chase is a dependent random walk with one line per page

page_cache.method: 2 GiB file on ext4 over virtio-blk; cache eviction via posix_fadvise(POSIX_FADV_DONTNEED) rather than drop_caches, so only this file is evicted

io_uring.method: 512 MiB file, random 4 KiB reads; io_uring driven through the raw io_uring_setup/io_uring_enter syscalls with queue depth 64, versus a pread() loop at queue depth 1

nice_weights.method: two spin loops pinned to the same logical CPU for 4 s; CPU time from getrusage; weight ratio from sched_prio_to_weight[] in kernel/sched/core.c

seccomp.method: raw prctl(PR_SET_SECCOMP, SECCOMP_MODE_FILTER); medians over 11 process runs of 500k raw getpid syscalls each

locality_trace.note: 85% of references fall in an 8-page hot set that moves every 2000 refs

gpu_transfer.conditions: H100 80GB HBM3, GPU 0 shared with another tenant at 70-90% SM utilisation; PCIe path, torch 2.7.0
gpu_transfer.method: torch.Tensor.copy_ of a 256 MiB float32 buffer, median of 15 timed copies with explicit cuda.synchronize on both sides
